citations.doc_title: Advances in Battery Technology: Rechargeable Magnesium Batteries and Novel Negative-Electrode Materials for Lithium Ion Batteries
Insertion Electrode Materials for Rechargeable Lithium Batteries
citations.authors: Martin Winter; Jürgen Besenhard; Michael E. Spahr; Petr Novák
citations.quoted_text:
Title: Insertion Electrode Materials for Rechargeable Lithium Batteries
Content: [975] J. Broadhead, H. F. Gibbard, H. Kuo, I. Chi, W. Bowden, in Progress
in Batteries and Battery Materials, Vol. 14 (Ed: R. J. Brodd), JEC
Press, Brunswick, OH 1995, p. 163.
[976] M.Jean,A.Tranchant,R.Messina,J.Electr...

Title: Advanced Energy Materials
Content: reviewed, as well as recent progress on the crucial cathode materials.

With the aim of electrifying portable devices and electric vehicles (EVs), rechargeable
lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) have attracted tremendous attention over the past two decades.1-5
They are not favourable power options for stationary energy storage, however, such as in
electrical grids, considering their relatively high cost, as well as their low energy and power
